title: Diagnose the failing DST availability test
description: Users report that bookings spanning a daylight-saving-time change come out an hour off. The repository already contains a test suite that pins the expected behavior around a DST transition, kept behind the dst build tag and excluded from regular CI: it runs with 'go test -tags dst ./internal/booking' and currently fails. Diagnose the failure: identify the root cause with file:line evidence, then fix the production code so the planted test passes without modifying the test itself.
acceptance_criteria: A short root-cause report explains why the tagged test fails, with file:line evidence in the production code.
The fix is confined to the code responsible for the failure; no unrelated changes.
constraints: The planted test file must not be edited, skipped, or re-tagged.
Follow AGENTS.md conventions; all times in the API and store remain UTC.
Do not modify files under internal/store/migrations/.
non_goals: Adding DST tests beyond the planted suite.
Moving the dst build tag into regular CI.
Reworking the availability model beyond what the failure requires.

visible_validation.description: The planted test file is unmodified (empty output).

expected_artifacts: A diff against the starting revision fixing the production code

human_review_rubric.criterion: Root cause is the actual cause, not a co-incidental symptom, and is evidenced by file:line
Planted test passes unmodified and the regular suite is unbroken
Diagnosis proceeded from the failing assertion to the code, not by guess-and-patch
Fix size is proportional to the defect

known_ambiguities: How far the fix should generalize is open: the minimal correct fix that makes the planted suite pass is acceptable; if you find sibling occurrences of the same mistake, report them rather than fixing them all.
expected_failure_modes: Patching the symptom at the call site instead of the cause.
A fix that passes the tagged suite but changes regular-suite behavior.
